priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer and affiliated entities have filed a definitive proxy statement with the SEC in connection with soliciting agent designations for a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi's common stock, and the filing advises all shareholders to read the proxy statement and other materials because they contain important information. The special meeting is to be held as promptly as practicable, although a specific date is not disclosed.

priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has issued a press release responding to recent media inquiries and SEC filings, calling on the Fermi Board to maximize shareholder value and find a new leader to replace the nine senior leaders who departed after his termination. Neugebauer highlights his team's accomplishments during his tenure, including securing a 99-year ground lease and over $1 billion in financing facilities, and emphasizes the need for transparency and accountability in determining Fermi's future. priority.title: SEC filing: DEFA14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO SECTION 240.14A-12)
priority.formExplanation: Additional definitive proxy material from the company — its side of an ongoing solicitation (responses, letters to shareholders, talking points).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc is urging shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card in response to former CEO Toby Neugebauer's consent solicitation to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders, citing Neugebauer's pattern of false claims and inaccurate statements. Neugebauer is seeking to replace the Board and leadership team with his own nominees, which Fermi's Board believes would jeopardize shareholder value and benefit Neugebauer and his affiliates at the expense of other shareholders. The company is highlighting its commercial momentum and progress under new management, and warning shareholders not to sign or return any GREEN Consent Cards sent by Neugebauer.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO 240.14A-12A)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, along with other participants, has issued a press release reaffirming his support for John Sellers of Double Eagle as Chairman and CEO of Fermi, citing the need for enhanced accountability and credibility to prevent value destruction by the current Board and management team. Neugebauer claims that the current Board has failed to replace nine key senior executives who departed Fermi after his termination and has ignored proposals from large companies that could offer greater value to shareholders. The release also highlights Neugebauer's intent to continue his campaign to seat a new, strong, independent Board and restore Fermi's position as a pro-shareholder REIT.

priority.title: SEC filing: DEFA14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO SECTION 240.14A-12)
priority.formExplanation: Additional definitive proxy material from the company — its side of an ongoing solicitation (responses, letters to shareholders, talking points).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc is mailing a Consent Revocation Statement to shareholders in response to a consent solicitation by former CEO Toby Neugebauer, who is seeking to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders. The company's Board of Directors urges shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card to protect their investment and support the company's continued execution of its Fermi 2.0 strategy. Neugebauer, who was terminated for cause, currently does not have the required support to call a Special Meeting, and the company emphasizes that it is making progress on its projects, including Project Matador, and is focused on maximizing long-term shareholder value.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO 240.14A-12A)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, the co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has filed definitive proxy solicitation materials to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders to elect a new Board majority committed to taking all actions to benefit shareholders. Neugebauer is seeking to replace the current Board with six independent directors who have no ties to the Neugebauer family or existing board dynamics, in order to consider a full-value strategic review and potential sale of the company. Shareholders of record as of June 10, 2026, are being asked to sign and return a GREEN agent designation card to support the call for the Special Meeting.

priority.title: SEC filing: PRRN14A — Fermi Inc. (REVISED PRELIMINARY CONSENT STATEMENT)
priority.formExplanation: Revised preliminary proxy material from a non-management participant — a dissident group's updated draft solicitation.
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder of Fermi Inc., is soliciting shareholder support to call a special meeting to vote on expanding the board of directors and electing seven new candidates, including himself, and removing three current directors. The proposed changes aim to install a new board majority that would consider a potential sale or strategic partnership of the company, which Neugebauer believes could maximize value for shareholders. Neugebauer's effort is a second attempt to provide shareholders with a vote on the company's future after his initial attempt was obstructed by the company.

priority.title: Fermi America™ Secures $165 Million Equipment Financing Facility from CSG Investments, an Affiliate of Beal Bank USA, to Accelerate Delivery of Six SGT-800 Gas Turbines for 2028 Delivery at Project Matador - PR Newswire
priority.summary: /PRNewswire/ -- Fermi Inc. (d/b/a Fermi America) (NASDAQ & LSE: FRMI), operating as Fermi America™, today announced that it has secured a $165 million senior...
priority.excerpt: AMARILLO, Texas , March 27, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Fermi Inc. (d/b/a Fermi America) (NASDAQ & LSE: FRMI), operating as Fermi America™, today announced that it has secured a $165 million senior secured, first lien delayed draw term loan from CSG Investments, Inc., an affiliate of Beal Bank USA, to finance the remaining progress payments on its previously ordered six Siemens Energy SGT-800-57 gas turbines, with delivery scheduled in 2028. The Borrower of the facility is Fermi Turbine Warehouse II LLC, a wholly owned special purpose entity of Fermi Inc.

priority.title: World's Largest Private Grid, Fermi America™ to File Additional 5GW Clean Air Permit for Project Matador with the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) Upsizing Campus Projections to 17GW of Private Power - PR Newswire

priority.excerpt: AMARILLO, Texas , March 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Fermi Inc. (d/b/a Fermi America) (NASDAQ & LSE: FRMI), operating as Fermi America™, in partnership with the Texas Tech University System (TTU System), today announced its intention to file an additional 5GW Clean Air Permit with the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ). The announcement made right on the heels of Project Matador's first 6GW Clean Air Permit, approved two weeks ago, signals the campus' plan to achieve ~17GW of total power including 11GW of clean natural gas, 4.4GW of nuclear energy, solar and battery sources.

priority.excerpt: SAN FRANCISCO, Feb. 25, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- National shareholder rights law firm Hagens Berman is issuing an updated notice to investors in Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ: FRMI) regarding the March 6, 2026, lead plaintiff deadline in a pending securities class action against Fermi, certain of Fermi’s top executives and directors, and underwriters of Fermi’s Initial Public Offering (IPO). The litigation alleges that Fermi misrepresented the demand for its flagship “Project Matador” —a massive AI data center campus—and the stability of its primary anchor tenant. priority.excerpt: AMARILLO, Texas , Feb. 11, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Fermi Inc. (d/b/a Fermi America) (Nasdaq & LSE: FRMI), operating as Fermi America™ , today highlighted continued progress in its strategic partnership with Hyundai Engineering & Construction Co., Ltd. (Hyundai E&C) to help restart large-scale nuclear construction in the United States, including ongoing Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) work supporting four AP1000 units planned for Fermi America's Project Matador, an 11-gigawatt private energy campus outside Amarillo, Texas. priority.excerpt: AMARILLO, Texas , Feb. 10, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Fermi Inc. (d/b/a Fermi America) (Nasdaq & LSE: FRMI), operating as Fermi America™ , in partnership with the Texas Tech University System, announced today that it has secured a $500 million financing commitment from MUFG Bank, Ltd. (MUFG), one of the 10 largest global financial groups. MUFG's financing serves as a solid institutional backing of Fermi America's strategy and further strengthens the ability to deliver the first 2.3 of 11 gigawatts of long-duration, reliable power at scale. As AI and advanced computing rapidly expand across the United States, one issue is becoming unavoidable: power.
